command_examples: PS C:\>.\ADRecon.ps1 -Protocol ADWS -DomainController <IP or FQDN> -Credential <domain\username> -Collect Domain, DomainControllers
Domain Controller IP Address or Domain FQDN.
Path for ADRecon output folder to save the CSV/XML/JSON/HTML files and the ADRecon-Report.xlsx. (The folder specified will be created if it doesn't exist) (Default pwd)

usage_sections: Getting Started: These instructions will get you a copy of the tool up and running on your local machine.

Examples: To run ADRecon on a domain member host. To run ADRecon on a domain member host as a different user. To run ADRecon on a non-member host using LDAP. To run ADRecon with specific modules on a non-member host with RSAT. (Default OutputType is STDOUT with -Collect parameter) To gener

arguments.name: -Collect
arguments.description: Which modules to run (Comma separated; e.g Forest,Domain. Default all except Kerberoast)

arguments.name: -DomainController
arguments.description: Domain Controller IP Address or Domain FQDN.

arguments.name: -GenExcel
arguments.description: Path for ADRecon output folder containing the CSV files to generate the ADRecon-Report.xlsx. Use it to generate the ADRecon-Report.xlsx when Microsoft Excel is not installed on the host used to run AD

arguments.name: -OutputDir
arguments.description: Path for ADRecon output folder to save the CSV/XML/JSON/HTML files and the ADRecon-Report.xlsx. (The folder specified will be created if it doesn't exist) (Default pwd)

arguments.name: -OutputType
arguments.description: Output Type; Comma seperated; e.g CSV,STDOUT,Excel (Default STDOUT with -Collect parameter, else CSV and Excel).

arguments.name: -PageSize
arguments.description: The PageSize to set for the LDAP searcher object. (Default 200)

arguments.name: -PassMaxAge
arguments.description: Maximum machine account password age. (Default 30 days)

arguments.name: -Protocol
arguments.description: Which protocol to use; ADWS (default) or LDAP

arguments.name: -Threads
arguments.description: The number of threads to use during processing objects (Default 10)

arguments.name: --target
arguments.description: Target host, IP, MAC, or URL the tool operates on.

arguments.name: --output
arguments.description: Output file path for the report or capture.

arguments.name: --version
arguments.description: Print the tool version and exit.

arguments.name: --wordlist
arguments.description: Path to a wordlist (users, passwords, paths).

arguments.name: --dry-run
arguments.description: Print what would be done without making any changes.

arguments.name: --force
arguments.description: Force the action even if a safety check would block it.

chain_examples.note: Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with zynon/LdapRelayScan (score=10); pair in the chain planner.

Phase 3: same attack_surface=['web', 'ad'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [web, ad] in the microsoft category. Fits when the engagement needs: Download from https://github.com/adrecon/ADRecon.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: Download from https://github.com/adrecon/ADRecon.
documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: git clone https://github.com/sense-of-security/ADRecon.git. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `PS C:\> .\ADRecon.ps1`. Further invocations from upstream: `PS C:\>.\ADRecon.ps1 -DomainController <IP or FQDN> -Credential <domain\username>`; `PS C:\>.\ADRecon.ps1 -Protocol LDAP -DomainController <IP or FQDN> -Credential <domain\username>`. Download from https://github.com/adrecon/ADRecon.

name: ADRecon

trust.warning: Attribution / index entry only. The operator must audit the code, the README, the license, and the releases before invoking. Phase 5 — entry is auto-generated from the operator's curated fetch list; no automated enrichment yet.

use_cases: When you need microsoft, use ADRecon because Download from https://github.com/adrecon/ADRecon.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `PS C:\> .\ADRecon.ps1` as documented upstream for ADRecon.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `PS C:\>.\ADRecon.ps1 -DomainController <IP or FQDN> -Credential <domain\username>` as documented upstream for ADRecon.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `PS C:\>.\ADRecon.ps1 -Protocol LDAP -DomainController <IP or FQDN> -Credential <domain\username>` as documented upstream for ADRecon.

When you need a concrete invocation, run `PS C:\>.\ADRecon.ps1 -Protocol ADWS -DomainController <IP or FQDN> -Credential <domain\username> -Collect Domain, DomainControllers` as documented upstream for ADRecon.
When chaining microsoft tools, pair ADRecon with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `ADRecon --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
